rationale: The cited paper is discussed in detail, not just listed: the authors summarize Dietvorst et al.'s findings on salesperson theory of mind and associated neural activation. They then use it to motivate specific late-stage neural hypotheses (e.g., TPJ/STS-related ToM processing) in their own study of deceptive ads. In the discussion, they again use Dietvorst et al. to position implications about individual differences in ToM and susceptibility to deception, showing an ongoing functional role in framing and interpretation.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Dietvorst et al. by moving ToM-based marketing neuroscience from salesperson interactions to consumer processing of deceptive advertising. It uses Dietvorst et al.'s ToM-neural evidence as a basis for predicted regions and then develops that line in a new marketplace context.
